Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Nov-29-2021 Continued To Feb-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught, via ZOOM, for Patricia Maravilla Tablante, Juliet Gil, and Victor Tablante, Respondents.

Counsel reports that the matter has settled with the court's Settlement Officer Pang Ly, and the parties are working to finalize the settlement terms within the next few weeks.

The Amended Petition, Case Management Conference, and Status on Judicial Mediation are continued to Feb-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Continued To Nov-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Continued To Nov-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Melbostad, via ZOOM, for Raquel Maravilla, Petitioner; Jon Vaught, via ZOOM, for Patricia Tablante, Juliet Gil, and Victor Tablante, Repsondents.

The court queries counsel regarding whether they are interested in participating in the court's judicial mediation program. Both counsel report that they are willing to do so. The court directs counsel to contact ADR Administrator Elizabeth Kelber by Oct-13-2021 to schedule a judicial mediation and complete the mediation by Nov-19-2021.

The Petition to Contest, etc., and Case Management Conference are continued to Nov-29-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status of Judicial Mediation is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Continued To Sep-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Vaught, via ZOOM, for Patricia Maravilla, Juliet Gil, Victor Tablante, Respondents; Paul Melbostad, via ZOOM, for Raquel Maravilla, Petitioner.

Mr. Melbostad begins by apologizing to the court for not meeting the court's deadlines and did so because the parties have been focused on settling the matter. The court then queries counsel regarding how much of a continuance they are requesting. Mr. Melbostad requests sixty (60) days and perhaps an order to a judicial mediation if settlement discussions are not productive by the next calling of this matter.

The Petition to Contest, etc., and Case Management Conference are continued to September 29, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. Counsel is advised to file a Joint Case Management Statement by September 17, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Continued To Sep-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Apr-14-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for Respondents.

The Court noted that Mr. Melbostad and Mr. Goldstein are not present, and that it failed to send out a case management order. Mr. Vaught stated that he has had recent contact with opposing Counsel, that an amendment was filed and that he filed a response to the amended petition. Mr. Vaught also stated that they seem to be in the early stages of this estate because of the amendment and suggested a 60-day continuance to see if they can do any further mediation. The Court spoke of the fashion of the case management order that is going to be issued. The Petition to Contest Validity of Trust and Will, etc., and the case management conference are continued to July 21, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Vaught that it will issue an order that will require the parties to file a joint case management statement by July 9, 2021.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Apr-14-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Continued To Apr-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught, Esq., counsel for Patricia Maravilla Tablante, Trustee, Juliet Fil and Victor Tablante, beneficiaries, (appearing by video conference); Paul Melbostad, Esq., counsel for Raquel Maravilla, petitioner/contestant (appearing by video conference).

The Court queries Mr. Vaught as to whether he waives defects of notice of hearing. He so waives the defects.

At the Court's inquiry, Mr. Melbostad advises the Court that an amended petition was filed. Mr. Vaught advises that he will be filing a response to the amended Petition shortly.
After discussion, the Court sets the matter for case management conference on April 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Case Management Conference Statements will be due one week before the hearing. The Court will prepare an Order setting forth the requirements for the contents of the statement.

[Current matter continued to April 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.]

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Continued To Feb-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities With Appendix Of Proposed Changes; Declaration Of Paul H. Melbostad; And [Proposed] Order

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Nov-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Melbostad appeared by zoom for Raquel maravilla.
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Patrica Tablante.

Mr. Melbostad stated that after the Supreme Court decision on Barefoot v Jennings he prepared an amendment to the petition, that he asked to be allowed to file the amended petition, but Mr. Vaught refused, and that Mr. Vaught feels the petition is up for demurrer. Mr. Vaught stated that Barefoot has nothing to do with this case, that the Petitioner was never a Beneficiary of the trust and never a trustee, that they have a Sister who is sticking her nose in something she has nothing to do with and that Mr. Melbostad's Client doesn't have standing. The Court informed Mr. Melbostad that he needs to prepare the amendment and file a motion for leave to amend. The motion for leave to amend is set for November 12, 2020 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Contest Validity of Trust and Will, etc. is continued to November 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Not reported.

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Paul Melbostad for Petitioner.
Jon R. Vaught for Respondent via Court Call.

Mr. Melbostad stated they had a full day mediation, that he did not get a status report filed, there is issue with regards to tenancy, and would like to request a 60-90 days continuance. Mr Melbostad further stated that he requested the original trust document be produce and after inspection, the case will settle one way or another. Mr. Vaught agrees with the continuance request to allow time for the document examiner to inspect and have a report.

The Amended Petition to Contest Validity of Trust and Will, etc., is continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint status statement to be filed by January 27, 2020.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Contest Validity Of Trust And Will, For Damages And Other Redmedies For Conversion And Financial Elder Abuse, For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Melbostad for Raquel Maravilla (Petitioner).
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Patricia Tablante, Victor Tablante and Juliet Gil.

Mr. Vaught stated that he filed a response to the petition. Mr. Melbostad stated that he received it, that the notices and acknowledgement of receipt have been filed, that he may need to take a few depositions and that he believes the matter could be set for mediation. Mr. Vaught stated that his Clients are anxious to get this resolved and that he is amenable to mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 3, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 4, 2019. The Petition to Contest Validity of Trust and Will, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on September 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
